title: How a Nature Cruise Turned Into a Nightmare
summary: The hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius set off alarms for a world still traumatized by Covid. For those on board, the danger was much closer.

title: With Possible Raúl Castro Indictment, U.S. Eyes Venezuela Playbook
summary: Amid stepped-up surveillance flights, a visit of the C.I.A. director and an energy embargo, the White House is trying to increase pressure on Cuba.

title: The Push to Indict Raúl Castro, Cuba’s Former President
summary: Mr. Castro, Cuba’s former president, could soon face charges in the 1996 killings of four volunteer airmen who were members of a humanitarian group that searched for migrants at sea.

title: Rubio, Once a China Hawk, Strikes Softer Tone to Align With Trump
summary: As a senator, Marco Rubio even hinted at the need for regime change in China. Now he talks about cooperation.

title: Trump Turns Taiwan Arms Sales Into Bargaining Chip With China
summary: The president said a potential arms deal for Taiwan was a “very good negotiating chip” in talks with Beijing. His words raise questions about the steadfastness of U.S. support.

title: China Will Host Putin, Days After Trump’s Visit
summary: The Russian leader will go to Beijing on Tuesday for talks with President Xi Jinping. The Kremlin said he had watched President Trump’s visit closely.

title: In Congress, a Sexual Harassment ‘Minefield’ Persists, Aides and Former Staff Say
summary: Nearly 10 years after Congress instituted measures to make it easier for women to lodge harassment complaints, lawmakers and aides say the behavior is still rampant.

title: What to Watch in Saturday’s Republican Senate Primary in Louisiana
summary: Senator Bill Cassidy, who has drawn President Trump’s ire, is fighting for political survival.

title: Political Money Is Flowing to Influencers. But From Whom?
summary: Social media stars have become a magnet for campaigns and political groups that want to push priorities without disclosing where their money is going.

title: Supreme Court Rejects Virginia Democrats’ Effort to Reinstate New Voting Map
summary: State officials had asked the justices to step in to allow the state to use a congressional map in the midterms that was drawn by Democrats and recently approved by voters.

title: Which Countries Are Profiting From the Iran War Oil Shock
summary: An analysis of oil export data offers clues about which nations have benefited from higher prices, and which have lost a lot of revenue.

title: Israel Says It Killed Hamas’s Top Leader in Gaza
summary: Izz al-Din al-Haddad took over the group’s military wing in Gaza last year. Hamas’s official broadcaster appeared to confirm Mr. al-Haddad’s death, although the group has yet to formally comment.

title: They Fled to Safety in Palestinian Territory, Then Settlers Attacked Again
summary: Violent settlers are not merely clearing Palestinians from land under Israel’s control. They are attacking areas where Israel agreed to Palestinian self-governance.

title: Trump Says a Top ISIS Leader Was Killed in a U.S.-Nigerian Mission
summary: The leader, whom the State Department designated a terrorist in 2023, had been hiding in Africa, President Trump said.

title: Long Island Rail Road Strike Shuts Down Busiest U.S. Passenger Rail Service
summary: This is the first strike on the service in more than 30 years. It comes after three years of failed contract negotiations, two federal interventions and a volley of last-minute bargaining.

title: Long Island Rail Road Workers Are on Strike. Here’s What to Know.
summary: America’s busiest passenger rail service shut down on Saturday after unions and transit officials failed to agree on a new contract.

title: What A.I. Kant Do
summary: Could we English majors have a future after all?

title: The Great Political Realignment of 2026
summary: The country seems to be tiring of the Trump era. Now what?

title: Finishing School
summary: It’s graduation season, a time of aspiration and anxiety. Today is about the aspiration — the wisdom to be gleaned from great commencement addresses.

title: Plot Was ‘Targeting Heart’ of New York’s Jewish Community, Tisch Says
summary: Police Commissioner Jessica S. Tisch spoke after prosecutors said they had arrested a man planning to attack a Manhattan synagogue.

title: It’s Been a Wild Week in British Politics. What Happens Next?
summary: Nobody has yet challenged Prime Minister Keir Starmer for the leadership, but his leading rival, Andy Burnham, finally has a route to Downing Street.
